tool.name: list_findings_for_validation
tool.description:
List all currently-tracked vulnerabilities so the validator can iterate through them.
Call this at the start of the validator pass to get the full list of findings that need re-execution verdicts. Returns id, title, severity, endpoint/method, whether the finding has a PoC script, and a preview of that PoC.
severity_filter: optional comma-separated list of severities to include (e.g., "CRITICAL,HIGH") — typically you only need to re-validate critical and high findings; medium/low are lower priority for validation budget.

parameter.name: severity_filter
parameter.description: Comma-separated severity names to include. Empty = all severities.

returns.description: total count, findings list (id, title, severity, endpoint, method, has_poc, poc_preview, target).

tool.name: validate_single_finding
tool.description:
Re-execute a single finding's PoC command and produce a scored verdict.
This is the core of the validator pass: takes a PoC command and three marker lists, runs the command in the sandbox, scores the output against the markers, and returns one of five verdict levels.
Verdict levels (first-match-wins): - CONFIRMED (0.75-0.95 confidence): exploit reproduces cleanly, expected markers present in output - POTENTIAL (0.50 confidence): 2xx response with meaningful body but no markers to pin down success - UNREPRODUCED (0.70 confidence): some error indicators present, no success signal - FALSE_POSITIVE (0.80 confidence): multiple error indicators, zero success signal - INCONCLUSIVE (0.30 confidence): neither success nor error indicators matched
Marker tuning guidance per vulnerability class: - LFI /etc/passwd: expected_markers=["root:x:0:0", "nobody:"] - RCE id: expected_markers=["uid=", "gid="] - XSS: expected_markers=[unique_random_token_you_injected] - Time-based blind SQLi: success_indicators=["sleep completed", "5.1", "5.2"] based on the expected delay - Reflected SQLi error: expected_markers=["sql syntax", "mysql", "postgresql", "ora-"]
After getting the verdict, call record_validation_verdict to persist it against the finding.

parameter.name: finding_id
parameter.description: ID of the finding being validated (from list_findings_for_validation).

parameter.name: poc_command
parameter.description: Shell command to re-execute the PoC. Typically a curl / python / nuclei / sqlmap invocation.

parameter.name: expected_markers
parameter.description: Strings that MUST appear in output for CONFIRMED verdict. Be specific (unique tokens, known file contents).

parameter.name: success_indicators
parameter.description: Generic success signals. Defaults cover common cases (200 OK, root:x:, shell prompts).

parameter.name: error_indicators
parameter.description: Signals that suggest failure. Defaults cover 403/401/404/WAF block/access denied patterns.

parameter.name: timeout
parameter.description: Max seconds to wait for the PoC to complete. Default 60.

returns.description: finding_id, title, verdict, confidence, reasons, exit_code, duration_sec, response_status (parsed from HTTP response line), output_preview, output_truncated, poc_command.

tool.name: record_validation_verdict
tool.description:
Persist a validation verdict against a finding in the tracer for the final report.
Call after validate_single_finding. The verdict and evidence are attached to the existing vulnerability record so the final report shows validation status next to each finding.
Rules for the reporting agent: - CONFIRMED findings → include with full details, no caveats - POTENTIAL findings → include but mark clearly as "potential, not fully reproduced" - UNREPRODUCED findings → include with explicit "could not reproduce during validation" note - FALSE_POSITIVE findings → DROP from final report entirely - INCONCLUSIVE → include with "inconclusive validation" warning, let client decide
If the tracer does not have a native verdict setter, the tool writes to /workspace/validation/verdicts.jsonl as an audit trail.

parameter.name: finding_id
parameter.description: ID of the finding being updated.

parameter.name: verdict
parameter.description: One of: CONFIRMED, POTENTIAL, UNREPRODUCED, FALSE_POSITIVE, INCONCLUSIVE.

parameter.name: reasons
parameter.description: List of reason strings explaining the verdict.

parameter.name: evidence
parameter.description: Evidence snippet (output preview, response body excerpt) to attach.

returns.description: finding_id, verdict, persisted flag, audit_file path if fallback was used.
